intro: Daniel (meaning God is my judge) is the name of several men in Scripture, most prominently the fourth-century prophet whose life and visions are recorded in the book bearing his name. Born of noble or royal Judean lineage, he was among the young men taken to Babylon by Nebuchadnezzar after the first deportation in 605 B.C. (Daniel 1:1–4). He distinguished himself in the Babylonian court by refusing to defile himself with the king's food, and through divine gift he interpreted dreams and visions that confounded the Chaldean sages (Daniel 2; 4). Under Belshazzar he interpreted the writing on the wall (Daniel 5), and under Darius the Mede he was cast into the lions' den for praying to God against the king's decree, emerging unharmed (Daniel 6). His four apocalyptic visions (Daniel 7–12) contain prophecies cited in the New Testament by Jesus, Paul, and John. Jesus refers to Daniel's "abomination of desolation" in the Olivet Discourse (Matthew 24:15).
hitchcock_meaning: judgment of God; God my judge
